title: Upper Attention Decomposition Audit
research_question: Now that the lower stack is simplified, how much full global attention does the upper stack really need, and which parts of the remaining upper attention machinery are still essential?
implementation_notes: Implemented via UPPER_ATTN_PLAN, which derives new upper-stack mixer-vs-attention layouts from the current hybrid frontier base.
ATTNRES_PLAN adds the narrow top-only routing variant needed for the combo candidate.
This avoids repeating the dead plain-local-window family and instead questions which upper layers still need full global attention.

candidates.name: aa1-only-top3-global-attention
candidates.goal: Test whether the upper-middle attention layer is less essential than the final three reasoning layers.
candidates.hypothesis: The model may only need the top three layers as true global attention reasoners.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the cleanest first test of how little global attention the upper stack really needs.
candidates.description: keep only the top three layers as full global-attention layers and simplify the rest of the upper stack

candidates.name: aa2-only-top2-global-attention
candidates.goal: Push the upper-attention minimalism story harder.
candidates.hypothesis: Most true global reasoning may happen only at the very top two layers.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the surviving upper attention stack can be thinned further without collapse.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the bold version of the same architectural question, not just a nearby tweak.
candidates.description: keep only the top two layers as full global-attention layers and simplify the rest of the upper stack

candidates.name: aa3-top2-global-plus-top-routing
candidates.goal: Test whether sparse upper attention works better when coupled to top-only routing.
candidates.hypothesis: Once only a tiny set of global attention layers remains, top-only routing helps select the right depth information.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether top-of-stack routing is part of the final reasoning stage rather than a separate side mechanism.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the one justified interaction test inside the upper-attention question.
candidates.description: use only two upper global-attention layers and add a top-only narrow routing module

candidates.name: aa4-interleave-upper-attn-and-mixer
candidates.goal: Test whether periodic global refresh is enough instead of contiguous upper attention.
candidates.hypothesis: Interleaving upper attention and lighter upper mixer/refinement blocks may preserve enough global reasoning.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether periodic global refresh beats dense upper attention.
candidates.why_worthy: This tests a distinct topology idea rather than just removing one more attention layer.
candidates.description: interleave global-attention and lighter refinement blocks through the upper stack

candidates.name: aa5-single-final-reasoner
candidates.goal: Test the strongest output-facing specialization story.
candidates.hypothesis: The model may want one dominant final reasoning layer more than many medium-strength upper attention layers.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the very last layer is doing a qualitatively different job from the rest of the upper stack.
candidates.why_worthy: This directly questions the assumption that the final attention stage should be spread across many layers.
candidates.description: use one final rich global-attention reasoner layer with lighter upper pre-reasoning layers below it
